title: pachycereus pecten-aboriginum subsp. tehuantepecanus
intro: Pachycereus pecten-aboriginum subsp. tehuantepecanus, also known as the Tehuantepec columnar cactus or 'Tepeztate', is a species of cactus native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of P. pecten-aboriginum subsp. tehuantepecanus include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestion, making it a valuable resource for various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to support overall well-being.
